title: Governor
text:
Definition:
The Governor is the constitutional head of a State and acts as the nominal executive.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 153, 155, 156, 157, 158, 159, 160, 161, 163, 167, 174, 175, 176, 200, 201, 213, 356.

Key Functions:
• Appoints the Chief Minister.
• Appoints other Ministers on the advice of the Chief Minister.
• Summons, prorogues and dissolves the State Legislature.
• Gives assent to Bills.
• Can reserve Bills for the consideration of the President.
• Promulgates Ordinances under Article 213.
• Acts as a constitutional link between the Union and the State.

UPSC Focus:
Role in Centre-State relations, discretionary powers, misuse of Article 356, Sarkaria Commission and Punchhi Commission recommendations.

title: President
text:
Definition:
The President is the constitutional head of the Union and the first citizen of India.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 52-78, 111, 123, 352, 356, 360.

Key Functions:
• Executive head of the Union.
• Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces.
• Summons and prorogues Parliament.
• Gives assent to Bills.
• Promulgates Ordinances.
• Declares National, State and Financial Emergencies.

UPSC Focus:
Constitutional position, discretionary powers, emergency provisions and legislative role.

title: Prime Minister
text:
Definition:
The Prime Minister is the real executive head of the Union Government.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 74, 75 and 78.

Key Functions:
• Heads the Council of Ministers.
• Advises the President.
• Coordinates government policies.
• Leader of the Lok Sabha or Rajya Sabha.
• Principal spokesperson of the Government.

UPSC Focus:
Coalition governments, cabinet system, Prime Minister's Office (PMO) and executive leadership.

title: Council of Ministers
text:
Definition:
The Council of Ministers assists and advises the President.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 74 and 75.

Key Features:
• Collective responsibility to the Lok Sabha.
• Headed by the Prime Minister.
• Includes Cabinet Ministers, Ministers of State and Deputy Ministers.

UPSC Focus:
Collective responsibility, cabinet government and executive accountability.

title: Parliament
text:
Definition:
Parliament is the supreme legislative body of India consisting of the President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 79-122.

Functions:
• Law making.
• Budget approval.
• Executive oversight.
• Constitutional amendments.
• Representation of the people.

UPSC Focus:
Parliamentary committees, disruptions, anti-defection law and legislative effectiveness.

title: Lok Sabha
text:
Definition:
Lok Sabha is the House of the People.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 81-104.

Key Functions:
• Directly elected House.
• Controls the Executive.
• Passes Money Bills.
• Approves Budget.
• Can remove the Council of Ministers through a no-confidence motion.

UPSC Focus:
Money Bills, Speaker's powers and parliamentary accountability.

title: Rajya Sabha
text:
Definition:
Rajya Sabha is the Council of States representing the States of India.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 80-104.

Key Functions:
• Permanent House.
• Reviews legislation.
• Represents States.
• Can authorize Parliament to legislate on State List subjects under Article 249.

UPSC Focus:
Federalism and bicameralism.

title: Speaker
text:
Definition:
The Speaker presides over the Lok Sabha.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 93-97.

Key Functions:
• Presides over House proceedings.
• Maintains order.
• Decides Money Bill certification.
• Decides disqualification under the Tenth Schedule.

UPSC Focus:
Neutrality of the Speaker and anti-defection cases.

title: Supreme Court
text:
Definition:
The Supreme Court is the highest judicial authority in India.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 124-147.

Jurisdiction:
• Original.
• Appellate.
• Advisory.
• Writ jurisdiction.
• Judicial review.

UPSC Focus:
Judicial independence, judicial activism, PIL and basic structure doctrine.

title: High Courts
text:
Definition:
High Courts are the highest courts at the State level.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 214-231.

Functions:
• Writ jurisdiction.
• Appellate jurisdiction.
• Supervisory jurisdiction over subordinate courts.

UPSC Focus:
Article 226, judicial independence and access to justice.

title: Judicial Review
text:
Definition:
Judicial Review is the power of the judiciary to examine the constitutional validity of laws and executive actions.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 13, 32, 131, 136, 141, 142, 226.

Key Features:
• Protects constitutional supremacy.
• Safeguards Fundamental Rights.
• Invalidates unconstitutional laws.
• Forms part of the Basic Structure doctrine.

UPSC Focus:
Judicial activism, constitutional supremacy and separation of powers.

title: Judicial Activism
text:
Definition:
Judicial Activism refers to the proactive role played by the judiciary in protecting constitutional values and public interest.

Key Features:
• Expansion of Fundamental Rights.
• Public Interest Litigation.
• Protection of governance and accountability.

UPSC Focus:
Judicial overreach versus judicial restraint.

title: Judicial Accountability
text:
Definition:
Judicial Accountability ensures that judges remain independent while maintaining transparency, ethics and public confidence.

Key Issues:
• Judicial appointments.
• Judicial conduct.
• Transparency.
• Pendency of cases.

UPSC Focus:
Judicial reforms and accountability mechanisms.

title: Basic Structure
text:
Definition:
The Basic Structure Doctrine limits Parliament's power to amend the Constitution.

Key Features:
• Constitutional supremacy.
• Rule of law.
• Judicial review.
• Federalism.
• Separation of powers.
• Democracy.
• Secularism.

UPSC Focus:
Balance between Parliament and Judiciary.

title: Federalism
text:
Definition:
Federalism refers to the constitutional division of powers between the Union and the States.

Key Features:
• Union List.
• State List.
• Concurrent List.
• Independent Judiciary.
• Written Constitution.

UPSC Focus:
Cooperative federalism and competitive federalism.

title: Centre-State Relations
text:
Definition:
Centre-State Relations define the constitutional relationship between the Union Government and the State Governments.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 245-263, 355 and 356.

Dimensions:
• Legislative relations.
• Administrative relations.
• Financial relations.

Role of Governor:
• Constitutional link between Union and State.
• Communicates constitutional issues.
• Can reserve Bills for Presidential consideration.

Institutions:
• Inter-State Council.
• Finance Commission.

UPSC Focus:
Cooperative federalism, Governor's role, fiscal federalism, Sarkaria Commission and Punchhi Commission.

title: Inter-State Council
text:
Definition:
The Inter-State Council promotes coordination between the Union and States.

Relevant Constitutional Article:
Article 263.

Functions:
• Policy coordination.
• Interstate dispute discussion.
• Cooperative federalism.

UPSC Focus:
Strengthening Centre-State relations.

title: Finance Commission
text:
Definition:
The Finance Commission recommends distribution of financial resources between the Union and States.

Relevant Constitutional Article:
Article 280.

Functions:
• Tax devolution.
• Grants-in-aid.
• Fiscal federalism.

UPSC Focus:
Vertical and horizontal devolution.

title: Election Commission
text:
Definition:
The Election Commission of India is an independent constitutional authority responsible for conducting free and fair elections.

Relevant Constitutional Article:
Article 324.

Functions:
• Conduct elections.
• Electoral rolls.
• Model Code of Conduct.

UPSC Focus:
Electoral reforms and independence.

title: Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG)
text:
Definition:
The Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) audits Union and State finances.

Relevant Constitutional Articles:
Articles 148-151.

Functions:
• Audit government expenditure.
• Promote accountability.
• Financial transparency.

UPSC Focus:
Public financial management and accountability.
